metadata.title: DSA Reference
metadata.description: Comprehensive Data Structures and Algorithms reference for local AI lookup. Includes topics, short descriptions, operations, typical complexity, and subtopics.

introduction.description: What DSA is, importance, and where it applies (software, databases, networking, AI, OS).
notes: DSA provides efficient ways to store, access, and manipulate data in software systems and algorithms; widely used across software development, databases, networking, AI and systems design.

complexity_analysis.description: Time and space complexity, Big O / Theta / Omega, best/average/worst-case.

arrays.description: Contiguous indexed collection; supports random access and efficient iteration.
arrays.notes: Used for static lists, buffers, and as building blocks for other DS.

linked_lists.description: Nodes linked by pointers; supports efficient insertion/deletion at known nodes.

queues.notes: Priority queue uses heap internally for efficient min/max extraction.

heaps.description: Binary heap is a tree-based priority queue supporting extract-min/max.

hash_tables.description: Associative array mapping keys to values using hash functions.
hash_tables.notes: Performance depends on load factor and hash quality.

tries.description: Prefix tree for strings enabling efficient prefix queries.

trees.description: Hierarchical data structure; binary trees are most common.
trees.notes: Balanced trees maintain O(log n) height; used in databases and indexes.

graphs.description: Nodes (vertices) connected by edges; used to model networks.
graphs.notes: Choose representation by density; adjacency list for sparse graphs.

disjoint_set_union.description: Union-Find structure for maintaining disjoint sets.

operations.find: near O(1) amortized with path compression
operations.union: near O(1) amortized with union by rank

segment_tree.description: Range query and update structure over arrays.

fenwick_tree.description: Binary Indexed Tree for prefix sums and updates.

suffix_array_tree.description: Suffix arrays / trees for string queries.

binary_search.notes: Requires random access; useful for order statistics and pivot finding.

quick_sort.complexity: O(n log n) average, O(n^2) worst

divide_and_conquer.description: Split problem, solve subproblems, combine results.

greedy_algorithms.description: Make locally optimal choices aiming for global optimum.

dynamic_programming.description: Store overlapping subproblem results to avoid recomputation.

bfs.use_cases: shortest path on unweighted graphs, level order

dijkstra.complexity: O(E + V log V) with heap

bit_manipulation.description: Use bitwise operators for constant-space optimizations.

greedy_approx.description: Approximation via greedy for NP-hard problems

two_pointers.description: Scan with two indices; used for sorted arrays and substring problems.

sliding_window.description: Maintain window with properties (sum, frequency) for linear scans.

binary_search_on_answer.description: Binary search over a monotonic answer space.

dfs_backtracking.description: Use recursion and pruning for combinatorial search.

dp_states_design.description: Define state and transitions; optimize with memoization or tabulation.

graph_modeling.description: Model real problems as graphs (nodes, edges) to apply graph algorithms.

computational_complexity.description: P vs NP, NP-completeness, reductions, hardness.

parallel_and_external_algo.description: Algorithms for parallel systems and external memory models.

randomized_algorithms.description: Algorithms using randomness for simplicity or speed (e.g., QuickSort pivoting variants, randomized hashing).

interview_topics.hints: Start with clarifying constraints, choose data structure, analyze complexity, optimize memory/time tradeoffs.

format_guidelines.lookup_strategy: Chatbox should search by 'topic', 'subtopic', and 'tags' fields, and fall back to 'description' full-text matches.
